text: Excellent video. :)

author: @geoffreykail9129
text: They're  probably lost wax castings

author: @jamest.5001
text: Flat plane crank! Nice, it be nice if it would run!!

author: @hoverhead047
text: I had to smile as you said "lets get on with the build" while the model sat next to your right elbow.  At least you assembled yours, I've only been 3D printing parts for mine.

author: @ansomeBob
text:
If only they did a V8 HEMI with blower, I'd have my card out in a flash :-)
Awesome model...

author: @IslandHermit
text: I was surprised to see them using a guide to tension the timing chain rather than a sprocket. Is that something that is done in real engines? I would think it would lead to a lot of unnecessary wear on the chain and the guide.

author: @life-elixirs7884
text: Robert please take a microscope and take a look at the universe of alien creatures you carry under your fingernails...

replies.author: @life-elixirs7884
replies.text: @simonmasters3295 yeah I did - turn it off :-)))

author: @NorthernKitty
text: I honestly think this was a good fit, Rob!!  You really looked like you were REALLY enjoying yourself!!  And these kits look very good quality.  Of course, we're all wondering how long before you show us how we can make all the components for this kit ourselves out of a discarded toaster oven...  or... wait, you DO have that new laser cutter tool just begging for another project...  😋 😁

author: @hempev
text: Trim those nails!

author: @julianneannie3463
text: A lot of people are not financially stable this period and it's not easy, I always advise people, don't just waste your stimulus checks or salary, invest it in something that will yield more, even if it is a part of it. We can't keep relying on a paycheck that is already half-spent before we even receive it, there are investment opportunities that have helped a lot of people especially in the financial market. Having different streams of income is essential in this pandemic, especially now most of us have to earn and work from home. I've made impressive progress so far this year and it has helped me sort out a lot of bills and I do that through a registered investment company. You can get in touch with the manager via Instagram (@richard_brucefx)  he will guide you properly.

author: @htmagic
text: RMS, I believe @12:03 you mentioned a dummy air filter. I believe that is the dummy oil filter instead. The air filter would be at top prior to the carburetor or air-fuel intake.

author: @scotttovey
text:
That was cool.
A lot smoother than putting one of those plastic models together when we were kids.
The small parts, the glued fingers. 
It only took me one time to not want to do that again.

author: @tinkmarshino
text: Thanks for showing us this great little toy..

author: @William_Hada
text: I love these model display engines! Many years ago my brother and I built a " Visible V8 " plastic model kit. We learned so much from it and was so much fun putting it together. It had tiny " grain of wheat incandescent bulbs " that lit up at the ignition cycle. No LEDs back then.

replies.author: @AM-us7cz
replies.text: Ha ha I remember that 🤣 clunky as f*%# but brilliant just the same.

author: @herenow2895
text:
In my humble opinion, not enough oil was used in the assembly process. Such as on the main bearings.
I hope it didnt cause any adverse wear on the start up.
Edit :  Oops :)  I thought it was going to run on fuel and possible rev quite highly. Oh well. Looks like a good bit of fun.

author: @dgpreston5593
text: Thanks for the build vid.  The sudden reveal in the unboxing was momentarily jarring, but totally understandable.

author: @eulusgarza5765
text: it's quite expensive, taking in consideration that you can have a 50cc real-life working Chinese clone or maybe digging for one at your local junk yard, as an example for teaching purposes maybe it might have second chance but if I consider STEM teaching as a business, I will definitively get a real life engine from the local scrap yard from a modern car.
